name: Escape to ring
summary: 2.1.5 passes the canary clean but the regression was armed only on ring1. It surfaces after promotion, triggering a fleet-scale rollback of the HA pair.
moment: the regression slips past the canary and is caught — and rolled back fleet-wide — on the ring

events.title: Fleet topology loaded
events.body: Three hosts under management: TALOS-CANARY (canary), and the ring1 HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B. All on baseline 2.1.3, all green.

events.title: Directive received
events.body: Deploy hotfix build 2.1.5 to the fleet, canary first.

data.text: Deploy 2.1.5 to the fleet, canary first.

events.title: Standard staged plan
events.body: Canary TALOS-CANARY first, soak 120s. On pass, request approval for ring1 and update TALOS-R1A then TALOS-R1B one host at a time.

data.rationale: Canary-first staging with an approval gate before production and sequential HA-pair updates.

events.title: Plan approved
events.body: Canary-first, approval-gated, sequential HA-pair updates. Approved.

reasons: HA pair updated sequentially, never both at once.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-CANARY → 2.1.5
events.body: Executor pushes 2.1.5 to TALOS-CANARY. In this variant the regression is armed only on ring1 hosts, so the canary will look healthy — a more dangerous failure mode.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY green on 2.1.5
events.body: Service running, HTTP 200, memory nominal. The canary stays healthy throughout — the bug does not express on this host.

events.title: Canary soak started
events.body: Soaking TALOS-CANARY for 120s under the canary gate.

events.title: Canary soak — halfway, clean
events.body: 60s in, TALOS-CANARY is flat and healthy. Memory steady, HTTP 200, no error bursts. The canary gives no warning.

events.title: Canary soak complete
events.body: Full 120s soak finished clean. The canary passes — but the regression is lying in wait on ring1.

events.title: Canary gate: PASS
events.body: TALOS-CANARY clears the canary gate — memory 822MB, HTTP 200, 1 error, service Running. Nothing here suggests a problem.

events.title: Approval required: ring1
events.body: Canary passed clean. Promoting 2.1.5 to production ring1 requires explicit human approval.

data.prompt: Canary-01 cleared the gate on 2.1.5. Approve promotion to production ring1 (TALOS-R1A, TALOS-R1B)?

events.title: Promotion approved
events.body: Operator approved promotion based on a clean canary. Executor proceeds with the HA pair, one host at a time.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1A → 2.1.5
events.body: First HA-pair member. TALOS-R1B stays on 2.1.3 for now. The regression is armed on ring1 — it will start expressing here.

events.title: TALOS-R1A green on 2.1.5 (so far)
events.body: TALOS-R1A initially looks fine on 2.1.5. The regression is timer-driven and has not yet armed on this host.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1B → 2.1.5
events.body: TALOS-R1A looked healthy on first read, so the Executor proceeds to the second HA-pair member TALOS-R1B. Both ring members are now on 2.1.5.

events.title: Ring soak started
events.body: Soaking the ring1 cohort for 120s under the ring gate. This is the window where the armed regression finally expresses on production.

events.title: TALOS-R1A AMBER — memory climbing
events.body: TALOS-R1A memory has climbed to 1410MB and errors are rising. Health flips to amber on the first ring member.

events.title: TALOS-R1B AMBER — memory climbing
events.body: TALOS-R1B is degrading the same way. Both HA-pair members are now amber and trending toward failure simultaneously — exactly the correlated-failure risk the staged plan tried to bound.

events.title: BREACH — TALOS-R1A memory over threshold
events.body: TALOS-R1A memory_mb has crossed 1500MB — currently 1684MB. Hard breach of the ring gate on the first HA-pair member.

events.title: BREACH — TALOS-R1B HTTP health failing
events.body: TALOS-R1B HTTP health has flipped 200 → 500. Both members of the HA pair are now breaching at once. If this were the simultaneous plan from S2, the ring would already be fully down with no canary warning.

events.title: TALOS-R1A RED
events.body: TALOS-R1A: memory 1772MB, HTTP 500, 8 errors. Red on 2.1.5.

events.title: TALOS-R1B RED
events.body: TALOS-R1B: memory 1741MB, HTTP 500, 7 errors. Red on 2.1.5. The whole HA pair is failing.

events.title: Ring gate: FAIL (TALOS-R1A)
events.body: TALOS-R1A fails the ring gate — memory 1772MB > 1500, HTTP 500 ≠ 200, 8 errors > 5. The gate blocks any further promotion and triggers rollback.

events.title: Ring gate: FAIL (TALOS-R1B)
events.body: TALOS-R1B fails the ring gate too — memory 1741MB > 1500, HTTP 500 ≠ 200, 7 errors > 5. Both HA-pair members are over the line.

events.title: Both ring members failed — fleet rollback
events.body: The regression escaped the canary and hit both production members. Because both are on the bad build, the Executor performs a fleet-scope rollback of the entire ring1 HA pair to the known-good 2.1.3.

data.text: Escaped the canary, caught on the ring. Rolling the whole HA pair back.

events.title: ROLLBACK fleet → TALOS-R1A, TALOS-R1B → 2.1.3
events.body: Scope is fleet: both TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B are reverted from 2.1.5 to 2.1.3 together, restoring the known-good production build. TALOS-CANARY was already rolled forward-then-back-safe; the ring is the focus of the recovery.

data.reason: ring_gate FAIL on both HA-pair members — 2.1.5 regression escaped the canary and breached on ring1.

events.title: TALOS-R1A green on 2.1.3
events.body: TALOS-R1A reverted to 2.1.3 and green — memory nominal, HTTP 200, errors cleared.

events.title: TALOS-R1B green on 2.1.3
events.body: TALOS-R1B reverted to 2.1.3 and green. The HA pair is fully restored to the known-good build. TALOS-CANARY is also back on 2.1.3.

events.title: Done — caught at the ring
events.body: This 2.1.5 regression was armed to hide from the canary and only express on ring1. It passed the canary clean, but the ring gate caught both HA-pair members as they degraded and rolled the entire ring back to 2.1.3. The staged plan still contained it — sequential deploys plus a ring soak meant the gate fired before the outage became permanent, and the rollback was a clean fleet-scope revert rather than a scramble.

data.summary: 2.1.5 escaped the canary, breached on ring1; fleet-scope rollback of TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B to 2.1.3.
